quiz_title: AL — Load and Performance - Physiological Effects of Physical Load [EN]
description: Acute and chronic physiological effects of physical load and their role in performance development.

questions.text: What is an acute physiological effect during physical load?
questions.explanation: An acute physiological effect is the immediate bodily response occurring during or directly after loading.

questions.text: Which phenomenon is a typical acute response to physical load?
questions.explanation: An increase in heart rate and respiratory rate is one of the most characteristic immediate physiological responses to loading.

options.text: Increase in heart rate and respiratory rate
Increase in the number of muscle fibres

questions.text: Regular physical training can improve the efficiency of the cardiovascular system in the long term.
questions.explanation: True. Regular training induces chronic adaptation that improves the function of the heart and vascular system.

questions.text: What most characterises chronic physiological effects?
questions.explanation: Chronic effects are adaptations that develop over a longer period as a result of repeated loading.

options.text: They disappear immediately when loading ceases
Adaptations that develop over a longer period
They are associated with a single loading session only

questions.text: Which system responds directly to physical load with increased activity?
questions.explanation: The cardiovascular and respiratory systems immediately increase their activity to ensure oxygen and nutrient supply to the muscles.

questions.text: What is one fundamental effect of physical load on the muscular system?
questions.explanation: Physical load increases the demand on muscle work, and with regular training the muscles adapt to this loading.

questions.text: Fatigue is exclusively a negative phenomenon and has no role in training adaptation.
questions.explanation: False. Fatigue is a natural consequence of training load and, with adequate recovery, can be part of the adaptation process.

questions.text: Why does the intensity of breathing increase during physical load?
questions.explanation: Because the body's oxygen demand increases and there is a greater need to expel carbon dioxide.

options.text: Because the muscles' energy demand decreases
Because oxygen demand and the need for gas exchange increase
Because the lungs switch to rest mode

questions.text: Which statement most accurately describes the physiological role of recovery?
questions.explanation: During recovery the body restores equilibrium, and this is when the foundation for further adaptation is established.

options.text: Recovery is the foundation of restoration and adaptation
Recovery reduces the long-term effect of training
Recovery is only a psychological phenomenon

questions.text: What is one characteristic metabolic response during physical load?
questions.explanation: Energy expenditure and metabolic activity increase during loading so the body can meet the demands of work.
